name: Tri-Heal Max Protocol
subtitle: High-Dose Three-Peptide Healing Blend
overview: Tri-Heal Max is an enhanced healing peptide blend featuring a higher dose of TB-500 (25mg) combined with BPC-157 (10mg) and KPV (10mg) for a total of 45mg per vial. This formulation emphasizes TB-500's tissue repair and cellular migration properties, complemented by BPC-157's growth factor upregulation and KPV's anti-inflammatory effects. The higher TB-500 ratio (2.5:1:1) compared to standard healing stacks targets more significant tissue damage or accelerated recovery needs.
mechanism: Each peptide contributes distinct healing pathways: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4) enhances cellular migration, promotes actin polymerization, and supports blood vessel formation for tissue repair; BPC-157 upregulates growth factors (VEGF, EGF), accelerates wound healing, and provides gastrointestinal protection; KPV (alpha-MSH fragment) inhibits the NF-κB inflammatory pathway, reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines, and may improve injection site tolerability. Together, they address inflammation, tissue regeneration, and cellular repair.
keyBenefits: Enhanced TB-500 dosing for significant injuries
May accelerate recovery from surgery or injury
Targets both acute and chronic tissue damage

items.name: Significant Injuries
items.description: Higher TB-500 dose targets more severe tissue damage requiring enhanced repair.

items.name: Muscle Tears & Strains
items.description: TB-500 promotes muscle fiber repair and cellular migration.

items.name: Tendon & Ligament Repair
items.description: Combined collagen support and growth factor upregulation.

items.name: Wound Healing
items.description: Multi-modal support for surgical wound recovery.

items.name: Reduced Inflammation
items.description: KPV component reduces post-surgical inflammatory response.

items.name: Scar Reduction
items.description: Enhanced tissue remodeling may reduce scar formation.

items.name: Athletic Recovery
items.description: Accelerated return to activity after sports injuries.

items.name: Chronic Overuse Injuries
items.description: Address accumulated tissue damage from repetitive stress.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection is the primary delivery method. Can inject near injury site or systemically. The higher TB-500 content makes this suitable for more significant healing needs.

protocols.frequency: Once daily for first 2 weeks, then reduce

steps: Clean vial top with alcohol pad

keyBenefits: Can inject near injury site for local effects

interactions.notes: Tri-Heal Max already contains BPC-157 and TB-500 - do not stack.
Contains overlapping components (TB-500, BPC-157, KPV) - avoid combining.
Complementary healing mechanisms; GHK-Cu adds copper-peptide benefits.
Multiple anabolic/healing pathways - medical supervision recommended.
Different mechanisms; GH secretagogues may complement healing.
TB-500 affects blood vessel formation; monitor with blood thinners.

timeline.effects: Anti-inflammatory effects from KPV; initial healing cascade activation
Tissue repair processes accelerating; reduced inflammation
Advanced tissue regeneration; assess for cycling off

common: Injection site reactions (KPV may help reduce these)
stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions or signs of infection
contraindications: No clinical trials on this three-peptide combination
Active cancer (theoretical concern with growth-promoting peptides)

good: Third-party testing confirming all three components
Certificate of Analysis with ratios verified
warning: Research-only status - not approved for human use

references.title: Thymosin Beta-4 (TB-500) Tissue Repair
references.keyFindings: TB-500 promotes cell migration, blood vessel formation, and wound healing through actin modulation.

references.title: BPC-157 Healing Properties
references.keyFindings: BPC-157 upregulates growth factors and accelerates healing of various tissue types.

references.title: KPV Anti-Inflammatory Effects
references.keyFindings: KPV (alpha-MSH 11-13) inhibits NF-κB pathway and reduces pro-inflammatory cytokines.

references.title: No Published Studies on Tri-Heal Max Combination
references.keyFindings: Specific three-peptide combination has not been studied in clinical trials.

quickStats.typicalDose: 200-500mcg total blend (higher end for acute injuries)
